title: The one with the fertility test

lines.speaker: Teleplay
lines.text: Robert Carlock

lines.speaker: Story
lines.text: Scott Silveri
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's so weird, how did Joey end up kissing Charlie last night? I thought you'd end up kissing Charlie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I thought I'd end up kissing Charlie too ok? But SURPRISE!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I missed most of the party (pause) Charlie's a girl, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, she is this new professor of my department that I did not kiss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know why Joey had to kiss her! I mean, of all the girls at the party, GOD!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why do you care so much?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes Rachel, why do you care so much?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (worried) Be-cause Ross is the father of my child! You know... and I... want him to hook up with lots of women! (pause) I just... All I'm saying is... I don't think that Joey and Charlie have anything in common.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, they seem to have a shared interest in each other's tonsils...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, Joey and a professor! Can you imagine if they had kids and if the kids got her intelligence and Joey's raw sexual magnetism... Oh, those nerds will get laaaaaid!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, so... Ross, you're ok with all this? I mean...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it's no big deal. I mean, I just met her and I'm fine with it...
Joey and Charlie enter. Ross looks at her

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, God. I forgot how hot she was!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna get some more coffee.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, you know what? I'll come with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok. (they both go)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) So, a professor, uh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! She is cool, and she's so smart! Her mind is totally acrimonious (which, being Joey, he mispronounces "amonious"). (pause) That's not how she used it...?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (talking to Ross) I feel like I owe you an explanation. I don't ordinarily go around kissing guys at parties. I'm... well, I'm kind of embarrassed. I really hope you don't think less of me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm no! Think less of you! No, I don't think less of you. I mean, you saw someone you liked and you kissed them. I mean, those people who like someone and don't kiss them... those-those people are stupid, I hate those people.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You know, actually I'm a little surprised to myself. I mean, Joey is so different from the guys I usually date. I mean, they're all professors, and intellectuals, and paleontologists mostly, you know, very cerebral...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know the type.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, if you wanna grab a bite before work we'd better get acrimonious. No? Am I getting close?
Opening credits
Joey and Rachel's apartment

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys! Look what I just got. (she shows them a pair of slippers)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, OH! Wow, I love those! Where did you get them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I bought them off Ebay! They used to belong to the late Shania Twain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (after a pause) Phoebe, Shania Twain is still alive!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh... then I overpaid. (she goes to the bathroom)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, what's this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's a gift certificate to this new SPA in SOHO.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you can't show Phoebe this! She hates those corporate massage chains.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, why, now I can't get a massage? There are so many things that she disapproves of! I can't eat veal, I can't wear fur, I can't go hunting...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you wanna go hunting?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I would like to have the option!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (coming back from the bathroom) What's up? (she sees the gift certificate in Rachel's hands) Hey, Rachel!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you can't go there! You know how I feel about these "big massage places"! They're putting people like me out of business!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And she wants to go hunting, too!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, come on, I don't wanna waste it! It would be like throwing away a hundred bucks!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, this is not about the MONEY, ok? It's about... it's about corporate greed destroying our hearts and leaving us... the hollow shells.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care about any of that!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, do you care about friendship?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I feel really strongly about this, Rachel. Please, don't use this gift certificate. I'm asking you as a friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, not as a friend, Phoebe!! Fine, I won't use it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Promise?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I promise.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you. (she tears up the gift certificate)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I am going hunting!!
Monica and Chandler's apartment

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey honey! I missed you today!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. (they kiss) What d'you wanna do tonight?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well... Maybe we could... (he sweeps the stuff off the table and wordlessly invites Monica to have sex on it)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, trying to turn me on by making a mess? Know your audience! Besides, tomorrow we're doing those fertility tests and until then you need to keep your tadpoles in the tank.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We really need to take those tests?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, we've been trying to have a baby for over a year. I think it's a good idea to find out if everything's ok. Just a few routine tests.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I don't wanna do it in a cup!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the big deal?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's weird! In a doctor's office?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not ok to do it in a doctor's office but it is ok to do it in a parked car behind a Taco Bell?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (embarrassed) I cannot believe Ross told you that! (pause) And in my defense, it was a Wendy's!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, I don't wanna do this test either, but I really do think it's a good idea!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, ok. I'm sure that doctor's office can't be worst than on a class trip to the Hershey's factory!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (really embarrassed) OH!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah! RACHEL TALKS TOO!
Joey's apartment

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sipping red wine from a glass) Who says that wine has to cost more than milk!
somebody knocks the door, Joey opens and it's Charlie

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Heeey!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on in, how are ya?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I'm good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I offer you a drink?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Please, I've been crazed all day! I had a meeting with the Dean, and my syllabus for summer school is due and I'm writing the Foreword for a friend's book...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-oh. I hade a pretty hectic day at work too, today I had to open a door and go (looking scared) ohhhh!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: So I am just so excited to be here. And I can't wait to start exploring the city!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, if you need a tour guide... (point to himself)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, you mean it? That would be so fun!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, definitely, definitely. Ok, what do you wanna see first?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, well, we can go see the Chronos Quartet at the Avery Fisher Hall.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking puzzled and nodding) Ok!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: And there is a collection of Walt Whitman letters on display at the public library.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, yeah!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: And first, I have to see the MET!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, let me stop you right there. The Mets suck, ok? You wanna see the Yankees.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: No, no, no, not the Mets, the MET, singular!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Which one, they all suck!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: The museum!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking puzzled) I don't think so.
SPA massage center, Rachel enters

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to the receptionist) Hi there!

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: (in an affected tone) Hello, welcome to Lavender Day Spa SPA. How may I help you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi. I have a massage appointment under Rachel Green, and here is my gift certificate.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: This has been torn up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And... taped back together.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Ok well, I'll call you as soon as your massage therapist is ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Have a seat through the glass doors.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (imitating the receptionist's tone) through the glass doors.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Through the glass doors.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright-y then.
Phoebe enters the hall

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Phoebe, your next client's in the waiting room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok. Do we have to talk like that then they're not around? (She sees Rachel) Oh, no, no! Listen, is there someone who can fill in for me?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Sorry, everyone is booked!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But that woman can't know I work here. She's a friend of mine and I made this big stink about how awful this massage chains are.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Then why you work here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 'Cause it's good money! But that doesn't change the fact that this is an evil blood sucking corporate machine!

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Well, I think this is a great place to work!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (watching around and whispering) Ok, are they listening?
Central Perk
Joey walks in and moves towards Ross, who's sitting of the sofa

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Charlie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (annoyed) Oh, do you, do you really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I'm... I'm kind of having a little problem.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, if you don't know what the word "acrimonious" means, just don't use it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, look, you know Charlie, right? She's cool, she's funny, her body is soo...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get to the problem!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes. It's just that she's so much smarter than all the girls I've ever dated! Combined! I don't want her to think I'm stupid!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking down) Are you wearing two belts?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (checking) EH, what do you know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were saying you didn't want to seem stupid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, right, right, well, she wants to go to all this cultural places and I don't know how to talk about that stuff. You gotta help me out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I really don't want to get involved in you guy's relationship.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Please, c'mon, you're the smartest person I know and I really like this girl, ok, I don't wanna lose her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (after a short hesitation) Fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok. Let's see. Oh, you should take her to the MET!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Metsss!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no! The MET! The Metropolitan Museum of Art.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (realizing) Oh, that's what she meant! (pause) You know, if they're gonna shorten it, they should call it the MUSE! You know, short for museum, and avoid all the confusion!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, most of it it's a place packed with confused angry baseball fans!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, all right, so I'll take her to the MET.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, uh, uh, ok, there's this great rare bookstore on Madison Avenue. You know what? She loves architecture, you know what you should do? You should take a walk down fifth to the Saint Patrick's Cathedral and there there's this great little pastry shop that she'd love.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Geez, sounds like you should be going on this date!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But I'm not! (pause). You know what if you're in the mood for Thai food...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Slow down, you go way too fast. Ok? Just go back to the MET, ok?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got to tell me exactly what to do there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, when you walk in the museum, take the right, that's the antiquities wing. Ancient Egypt, Mesopotamia, up to the Byzantine Empire.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa! So, I walk in the door and make the right (and he bends his arm to the left. Ross then bends Joey's arm to the right and Joey nods)
doctor's waiting room

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have a weird feeling about this place. (pause) How do I know that they are not gonna secretly videotape me and put it all over the internet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because, honey, I mean this in the sweetest way possible, nobody is gonna wanna watch that.
a nurse walks in

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Mr. Bing? (Chandler jumps up) Here you are! You'll go into that room and deposit your specimen into the container.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Deposit my specimen? You know, usually I have to call a 900 number for that kind of talk. Thanks, got it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, honey, my test is down the hall, are you sure you're going to be ok?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I guess!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know this is embarrassing, but nobody cares! No one here even knows you!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: OH MY GOD!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, Come on!
Commercial Break

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ah ahahahhahaa! How great is this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, we're probably fertile, let's go home!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Well, Sid and I are trying again and we had trouble last time because apparently we...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Heaven) No no no... I mean, why? why is she here??

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh! Someone's a little cranky today cuz they have to do it in a cup! (laughs) Oh! They gave you the kiddy size (looking at the cup in his hand).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What!?
Janice does her "Janice Laugh"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This was fun! But I've got an invasive vaginal exam to get to! (leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd love to stay, but I have eh... (points at the cup) got a hot date... (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Please... go! (Then shouts after him) Just let me know if you need a hand!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (disgusted) I think it just fell off. (Leaves)
At the Spa, Phoebe is at the half-opened door

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (In a strange heavy accent) Hello "ja", it's time for your massage, ja! Put your face in the hole.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, a Swedish massage from a real Swedish person. (Puts her head in the hole and Phoebe enters)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, then I'm Swedish...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, what's your name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a normal Swedish name... Ikea...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... what an interesting name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ja!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know I... (lifts her head and tries to look in Phoebe's direction)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pushes her head back down) Time for your scalp massage!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Sees Phoebe's slippers through the hole) Wow... I really love your... (startled as she realizes those are Phoebe's slippers)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's just that uhm... it feels so good... Ikea... (pause) Yeah, say hey, you'll know this, what's the capital of Sweden?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Thinks for a few moments) Uhm... Stockholm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Damn! I wish I knew if that was right!
Joey's apartment, Joey and Ross in the living room, rehearsing what Joey will say to Charlie in the Museum

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (gesturing at an imaginary painting) Note the painterly lines and subtle impasto on this canvas. Monet painted quickly and usually outdoors as his elusive subject was light itself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, do you have any idea what you just said?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shaking his head as if to say: of course not!) No, no, my mouth says the words, my brain is thinking monster trucks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok now, remember, when you get to the museum, Monet is not spelt M-O-N-A-Y. I just... I wrote that out phonetically for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phonetically? (Looks confused)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah that means... you know? We just... we don't have time for this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, but you know what? I gotta say, I'm really impressed that you were able to memorize all this so quickly!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah! I'm an actor! I can memorize anything! Last week on "Days" I had to say "Frontal temporal zygomatic craniotomy".

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow. What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No idea! But the guy I said it to dies in the next scene so I guess it means "you're gonna get eaten by a bear".

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok! So let's move on to the Renaissance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, Caravaggio uses chiaroscuro here to highlight the anguish of the central figure. Touch it, it's really bumpy! (Reaches out to touch the imaginary painting).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nah ah! Nah ah! (stops him form doing so) No no no! No ad-libbing and dude, you can't touch the paintings.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! you... (reaches out to touch the imaginary painting again)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! (Slaps his hand)
The Fertility Clinic; Chandler walks out one of the rooms

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To the nurse at the nurses' station) My specimen is in the room and I just want to thank whoever knocked on the door while I was in there. Really helped speed the process along! (walks towards the common area and sees Janice is still there) Janice! You're not... gone?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh! Sid is still in his room. I don't allow porn at home so this is like a vacation for him. So did you do it? Did you make your deposit?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! yeah... The hard part is over!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: That's not the hard part honey! The hard part is what comes next, I mean aren't you worried about the results?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I haven't... I haven't even thought about the results yet... I just assumed that everything was gonna be ok.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh! Well, you know what? It probably is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Slightly panicky) Yeah, but what if it's not? What if there is a reason why we can't have a baby?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, Chandler, look. You and Monica are meant to have children. I am sure it's gonna be just fine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiling again) oh, oh, yeah, ok, thanks. I can't believe I didn't even think of that. I guess I was just so worried about having to... come here and do... 'that'...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What, you can do it in the parking lot of a Taco Bell, but you can't do it at a doctor's office?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (stares at her intently, then yells) It was a "Wendy's!! "
Phoebe and Rachel at the Spa. Phoebe is still massaging Rachel

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Singing) "Ipan Stripan, Glupi Glabi! " And that is the Swedish National Anthem! Thank you for asking! (looks annoyed)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow, Ikea... what a rich culture. Uhm, you know what? I have a friend who is a masseuse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Ja! Ja!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yah! She's... uhm... not very good though... (Phoebe looks devastated)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhu, uhu... and why do you think that is?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know... maybe it's because she has got such callousy fingers from playing crummy guitar...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or... maybe she has trouble loosening your knots because you're such a high maintenance tight ass!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (now lifts her head) Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know it's me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: For like a half an hour! Man, you can lie about Sweden!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How can you come here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How could you not tell me you worked here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't have to tell you everything!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes you do, if you're going to make me feel guilty for getting a free massage!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tips not included.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Phoebe, why did you lie to me about working here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I was ashamed ok? I sold out for the cash! And then they give me benefits like medical, and dental, and a 401K. But you know... you pay a price. Now I'm this Corporate stooge and punching a clock and Ugh! paying taxes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, honey, if you hate it so much, you should walk out there right now and quit! Be true to what you believe in! Honey, you have principles and I so admire that! I don't have any!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know what? You are right. I am gonna quit. It's time I took my life back!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good for you Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay (walks out and closes the door behind her, looks up and whispers) If you guys have microphones in there too, I didn't mean any of that. I love you.
Central Perk. Ross is playing some shoot'em up game on his laptop

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Haha! Got ya! Die, die, die!
Charlie walks in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (recovers his composure and starts typing) Respectfully, professor R. Geller. (hits <return>, closes the laptop and joins Charlie on the sofa) Hey!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, how was the Met?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: The museum was amazing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Joey really knows his art, huh?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Not so much, no. He had clearly memorized all the stuff to say, and some of it didn't even make any sense.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Well, for one, he was talking about paintings that were nowhere around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (perplexed for a moment) Wait a minute... when you guys walked into the Met, did you go to the right?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: No, we went to the left.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (shaking head) Oh Joey, Joey! But still, I mean, it seems like you guys are having a great time together.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah, it's fun (hesitating).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually, you know, Joey is your friend, and you don't really know me that well; it would be weird.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, I mean, a little, but no, what, go on.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Well... I'm just thinking that maybe he's not the right guy to be with right now, maybe I should be with someone... I have more in common with. You know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (slowly) Yeah. But you know what? I think you should give Joey a chance. I mean, he's a great guy, and sure he doesn't know that much about art but you know, you can always talk about that with someone else.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah, I guess that's true.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And if you think about it, I mean the reason he memorized all that stuff is because he thought it was important to you. You know, that's the kind of guy Joey is.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: He is very sweet. Plus he's hot!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was going to be my next argument.
Joey walks in

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Charlie) You're ready?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah, let's go. (stands up and kisses Joey) (to Ross) Thanks Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (aside, to Ross) Hey Ross! That art stuff worked, you hooked me up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Glad I could help man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Although some of that stuff wasn't where you said it was gonna be, but... (confidently) I made it work.
Monica and Chandler's. Chandler is sitting on the sofa, reading the newspaper.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (picking up a plastic cup similar to the one he deposited his specimen in) It is not okay that I'm aroused by this now.
phone rings

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello? Oh hi, Doctor Connelly. (pause) No, she's not here but, you know, I can tell her. Should I be sitting down for this? (his smile fades as he hears the answer) Oh. (pause) Well, so what does that mean? (pause) Ok. Ok, thank you. Thanks. (hangs up)
Monica walks in

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey sweetie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Doctor Connelly just called.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: With good news? (very quickly and wringing hands) Of course it is not good news, you just said (deadpan) "Doctor Connelly just called". If it was good news you would have said (excitedly) "Doctor Connelly just called! " But so what is it? Is there a problem, uh? Is there a problem with me or with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually it's both of us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Apparently my sperm have low motility and you have an inhospitable environment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh... what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It means that my guys won't get off their barcaloungers and you have a uterus that is prepared to kill the ones that do. (pause) It means...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (seriously) It means that we can keep trying, but there's a good chance this may never happen for us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (weeping) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry too.
they hug

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we're gonna... we're gonna figure this out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (still weeping) I know.
Closing credits
The Spa Reception. Phoebe walks in

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Good morning Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (imitating the receptionist's tone) Good morning receptionist.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Here's your schedule for the day. Your first client is in room No. 1.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel Green? (angrily) Son of a bitch, she came back?
Phoebe walks to the door and half-opens it

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (through the door, with a Scottish accent) Are you ready for your Scottish massage? Put your face in the hole, lassy.
End
